GCHWARRIOR 3Rd EDItIG 
COMBAT
In the war-torn BattleTech universe _ combat is constant fact of Iife. Sooner or later; as much as they might try to avoid It; MechWarrior characters will inevitably get into situation that words won get them out of; then the guns start blazing_ and all bets are off. Since the lives of the characters are on the line during com- bat, and sometimes the success failure mission can hinge fight' $ outcome_ additional rules are necessary t0 help the players and gamemaster determine what happens these tense ad fast-paced situations The following rules expand tne Game Concepts chapter 13) t0 provide an easy-to-play but fairly realistic persona com bat system that should be able to handle most 0f the situations that will arise in game play- The gamemaster should feel free to expand on (or cut from) these rules to cover unforeseen circum stances make these rules fit better into his own campaign (However. make sure the players are aware of any changes made to the published rules before you start using them )
character may never perform more than one movement action (walk, run; evade disengage or sprint) in the same turn_
Incidental Actions Incidental Actions require little or no attention physical movement: Although the rules allow any number of Incidental Actions the gamemaster may want t0 restrict characters t0 no more than five per turn t0 keep tne game moving: Delay Phase Drop Object Drop Prone Engage Opponent (in Melee Combat) Gesture Observe Speak (single word) Walking Movement
Simple Actions bit more involved than Incidental Actions; Simple Actions can be performed by reflex, or with only brief attention or move ment. The vast majority of things characters will do fall under the heading of Simple Actions: Careful Aim Lead Team Load Weapon Observe in Detail Pick Up/Put Down Object Ranged Attack Ready/Draw Equipment Recover from Stun Running Movement Speak (brief phrase} Stand Up from Prone Stow/Sheath Equipment Use Simple Object
Brlef Note About Reality should be noted that the combat system in Mw3 ~like the System of its cousin BattleTech is not intended t0 be simulation of reality: was created t0 resolve combat situations quickly and easily; and the results of attacks are intended t0 be somewhat realistic. not 'real-world accurate: Above all; fast fun was the main design goa of the Mw3 combat system and close second was making flexible enough t0 deal with the Mide range weapons and armor available to MW3 characters. 6 the specific wound results of specific gun against specit: armor at specific range just don' seem t0 match actual bal: listics data , the designer hopes that you will forgive the inaccu: racy favor of playability.
THE COMBAT TURN order to keep things organized combat action is broken up into series [urns each of which represents approxi- mately seconds of real time. During each turn every character will get an opportunity to act , called phase . During each character $ phase the char acter may take certain number of actions, depending on their complexity
Complex Actions Complex Actions require character's full attention_ and when per formed are the only Ihing character can do during that turn (aside from Incidental Actions of course): Disengage (from Melee Combat) Evading Movement Melee Attack Extinguish Fire Recover Fatigue Speak (conversation) Spot for Indirect Fire Sprinting Movement Use Complex Object Use Skill
ACTIONS character can only do s0 much in seconds_ To represent character limited to either one Complex Action Or [WO Simple Actions in his phase_ In addition, character can per form any number of Incidental Actions in his phase (within reason}. Specific   examples of each type of action are provided below: These guidelines should be used unless the rules specif- ically state otherwise For example loading weapon con" sidered Simple Action_ Tnere are some weapons that require Complex Action to reload but that will be included in the description of the weapon.
